---
title: "Website Traffic Analytics Codebook and Report"
format: html
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
---

## Purpose

This reproducible report documents the web traffic analytics framework for Lydia Chen's professional portfolio website.

## Research Question

Which traffic sources and pages are most effective at attracting, engaging, and converting prospective audiences?

## Data Dictionary

| Variable | Definition | Marketing Interpretation |
|---|---|---|
| date | Event or session date | Supports trend analysis |
| source_medium | Traffic source and medium | Identifies acquisition channel |
| page_title | Visited page title | Shows content interest |
| sessions | Number of sessions | Measures traffic volume |
| engaged_sessions | Sessions meeting engagement criteria | Measures quality of visits |
| conversions | Completed target events | Measures business outcome |

## Sample R Analysis Structure

```{r}
# Replace sample data with exported GA4 data.
library(tidyverse)

traffic <- tibble::tribble(
  ~source_medium, ~sessions, ~engaged_sessions, ~conversions,
  "google / organic", 420, 260, 24,
  "linkedin / referral", 180, 128, 18,
  "instagram / social", 260, 150, 12,
  "direct / none", 210, 110, 8
)

traffic_summary <- traffic |>
  mutate(
    engagement_rate = engaged_sessions / sessions,
    conversion_rate = conversions / sessions
  ) |>
  arrange(desc(conversion_rate))

traffic_summary
```

## Interpretation Template

- Best channel by traffic volume: identify the largest session source.
- Best channel by quality: compare engagement rate and conversion rate.
- Optimization priority: improve CTAs and reduce conversion friction on high-traffic pages.

## Limitations

- GA4 attribution may not show the full customer journey.
- Tracking accuracy should be validated before making budget decisions.
- Small samples should be interpreted directionally.
